channel.title: Tech Insights
channel.description: Daily curated news on AI, developer tools, and the tech industry

item.title: Meta made its own AI-generated clickbait news feed
item.description: Facebook has long been filled with feeds of clickbait articles. Now, Meta is making its own clickbait articles with AI. The standalone Meta AI app now has a "For You" section that populates a list...

item.title: Hello, dev.to — I'm Victor: 10+ years full-stack, two CTO runs, now solo and writing in the open
item.description: Hi. I'm Victor, a full-stack engineer based in South Korea, and this is my first post here. I've been writing for a while — just in Korean, on my own blog. I'm starting fresh in English on dev.to...

item.title: Rasa 播客谈对话设计的演变
item.description: 这段 Rasa 的播客视频（节目名为 The Dialogue Architects）中，主持人和嘉宾 Rebecca Evanhoe（《Conversations with Things》一书的共同作者，现任 Slang AI 的产品经理及对话设计负责人）围绕大语言模型（LLM）时代下对话设计的演变、语音 AI 在餐饮业的落地落地细节以及评估与优化方法展开了非常深入的探讨。...

item.title: OpenAI 推理模型如何成功推翻一个由著名数学家保罗·埃尔德什（Paul Erdős）提出的、长达 80 年之久的数学猜想
item.description: 在这期 OpenAI 播客中，主持人 Andrew Maine 邀请了来自 OpenAI 推理研究团队（Reasoning Research Team）的三位核心研究员：Alexander Wei、Hongxun Wu 和 Lijie Chen。他们详细分享了 OpenAI 推理模型如何成功推翻一个由著名数学家保罗·埃尔德什（Paul Erdős）提出的、长达 80 年之久的数学猜想...

item.title: Matt Turck 对话了OpenAI 强化学习基础团队（Foundations of Reinforcement Learning）主管 Dan Roberts
item.description: 在这次访谈中，主持人 Matt Turck 对话了 OpenAI 强化学习基础团队（Foundations of Reinforcement Learning）的主管 Dan Roberts。 Dan Roberts 拥有 MIT 理论物理学博士背景，曾研究黑洞、量子引力和量子混沌，后转向 AI 领域。节目围绕近期 AI 在数学领域的突破（如破解埃尔德什 Erdos...

item.title: 模糊测试（Fuzzing）——一种通过输入随机数据来查找软件漏洞的高效技术
item.description: 这期来自 Computerphile 的视频由帝国理工学院多核编程研究组负责人 Alastair F. Donaldson 教授主讲，深入浅出地讲解了模糊测试（Fuzzing）——一种通过输入随机数据来查找软件漏洞的高效技术 [00:00]。 教授将模糊测试的原理、应用场景以及极其重要的“覆盖率引导模糊测试”算法进行了全方位的拆解。以下是视频内容的详细总结： 一、...

item.title: I set up Claude Code for a real production project. Here's what actually earned its keep
item.description: Everyone's got a "10 AI coding tricks" post. This isn't that. This is what's left after three weeks of running Claude Code on a real project — a bilingual booking bot for a beauty salon (Telegram...

item.title: I'm Starting Over — And That Might Be My Biggest Advantage
item.description: Yesterday, I finished my 6th semester examinations. For most students, that's the end of a stressful academic phase. For me, it's the start of something more demanding — placement season. Here's a...

item.title: I Tested 17 AI Tools Side by Side — Here's What Actually Matters
item.description: I Tested 17 AI Tools Side by Side — Here's What Actually Matters A few months ago I got tired of bookmarking AI tools I never used. My tab bar looked like a hoarder's garage — a writing assistant...

item.title: I shipped the product. I have no idea how to launch it. Tell me about your first time.
item.description: For the past two weeks I've been head-down in code. 5400 lines refactored across 20+ PRs. Persistent job queue migrated to Postgres. Rate-limiting deployed. Voice-source pipelines fixed (two of...

item.title: On-Device Debugging And JUnit 5
item.description: This is the first follow-up to Friday's release post and it covers the two changes from this release that affect how you iterate on a Codename One app rather than what the app itself does....

item.title: Kabuto Park captures the fleeting joy of summer vacation
item.description: There are a lot of games that remind me of summer - hot days in the backseat with a copy of Dragon Warrior III, cooling off in the basement while grinding Gran Turismo races - but there aren't a...

item.title: Here comes new Siri again
item.description: Apple has been on its back foot, AI-wise, for the past few years. But in a strange way, playing from behind might not be such a bad move. At WWDC on Monday, Apple appears to be getting ready to...

item.title: The next YouTube phenomenon hitting the big screen
item.description: Hi, friends! Welcome to Installer No. 131, your guide to the best and Verge-iest stuff in the world. (If you're new here, welcome, happy last week of productivity before the World Cup starts, and...

item.title: Persona Atlas: Mapping How Famous Minds Think
Crypto-Funded Chinese Peptide Labs Are Booming
item.description: Plus: Hackers use Meta’s AI bots to hack Instagram accounts, Anthropic helps NSA hackers, a decades-long GPS satellite mystery may have been solved, and more.

item.title: Free Apps Are Quietly Turning Smart TVs Into Web-Scraping Proxies for AI
item.description: A researcher has reverse-engineered the iOS SDK that Bright Data embeds in consumer apps and documented how it turns devices, including always-on smart TVs, into exit nodes that relay web-scraping...

item.title: CISA Adds Actively Exploited SolarWinds Serv-U DoS Flaw to KEV Catalog
item.description: The U.S. Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security Agency (CISA) has added a high-severity security flaw impacting SolarWinds Serv-U multi-protocol file server software to its Known Exploited...

item.title: AI Agent Uncovers 21 Zero-Days in FFmpeg; Chrome Patches Record 429 Bugs
item.description: Two things landed within days of each other this week. A security startup reported 21 previously unknown vulnerabilities in FFmpeg, the media library inside almost everything that touches video,...

item.title: Miasma Worm Hits 73 Microsoft GitHub Repositories in Major Supply Chain Attack
item.description: Microsoft's GitHub repositories have become the latest to fall victim to the ongoing Miasma self-replicating supply chain attack campaign. The incident impacted 73 Microsoft repositories across...

item.title: Cisco Catalyst SD-WAN Manager CVE-2026-20245 Flaw Actively Exploited – No Patch Available
item.description: Cisco has warned that a high-severity security flaw impacting Catalyst SD-WAN Manager has come under active exploitation. The vulnerability, tracked as CVE-2026-20245, carries a CVSS score of 7.8...

item.title: More than a decade later, the team behind N++ is back with a multiplayer sequel
item.description: Back in 2015, the two-person studio Metanet released N++, a brutally hard 2D platformer that was a decade in the making, building off of previous releases dating back to the freeware Flash title...

item.title: Grand Theft Auto VI is warping the video game release calendar
item.description: Who's afraid of the next GTA? Based on the last few days of Summer Game Fest, just about everyone. Grand Theft Auto VI hasn't been present at any of the keynote events, but its presence was felt...

item.title: Final Fantasy VII’s remake trilogy will conclude with Revelation
item.description: Square Enix has officially announced the third and final game in its Final Fantasy VII remake trilogy: Final Fantasy VII Revelation. It will release on multiple platforms simultaneously - PC, PS5,...

item.title: Control Resonant is a sequel — and also a starting point
item.description: Chronologically, Control Resonant is a sequel to 2019's Control. But in most other ways, the games aren't directly connected. To developer Remedy, they're more like two sides of the same coin....

item.title: Congress still can’t decide what to do about warrantless surveillance
item.description: The deadline to reauthorize Section 702 of the Foreign Intelligence Surveillance Act is coming up a week from now on June 12th, and legislators seem no closer to reaching a deal. If this sounds...

item.title: How a USB-connected speaker can infect a PC without ever being touched
item.description: Seller of the Sound Blaster Katana V2X doesn't consider the behavior a vulnerability.

item.title: Startup Battlefield 200 applications officially close in 3 days
item.description: Applications for Startup Battlefield 200 officially close on June 8, 11:59 p.m. PT. Don't wait any longer. Secure your shot at competing on the Disrupt Stage at TechCrunch Disrupt 2026 this...

item.title: Google will pay SpaceX $920M per month for compute
item.description: In a statement, a Google representative described the deal as a result of unexpected demand for its recently launched AI products.

item.title: IronWorm and New Miasma Worm Variant Hit npm in Supply Chain Attacks
item.description: Multiple software supply chain attacks have hit the npm ecosystem, with threat actors using both malicious and poisoned versions of over 50 legitimate packages to distribute a Rust-based...
